mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 yields positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances following a >0.5pp unemployment spike in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive return frequency above 58% over 60-day horizons in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production below 1.0% signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress short-term equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of cases when industrial production growth falls below 1.0% across rolling test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stationary federal funds rate indicate unanchored inflation expectations that erode real equity returns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets generate positive 20-day returns in > 62% of periods where CPI rises above 0.3% monthly while policy rates remain unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue projections, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for payroll growth against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.015, or positive return frequency drops below 54%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real gross domestic product exceeding 0.5% triggers recessionary risk pricing that forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of quarters following a >0.5% GDP contraction in historical backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling housing starts indicates inventory accumulation without final demand absorption, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when industrial output rises while housing construction declines across walk-forward periods.
